basic_details.job_title: NHSRC Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Lead Consultant, Junior Consultant and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: National Health Systems Resource Centre (NHSRC) Consultant Notification 2026 has been released on the official website of NHSRC. Multiple Consultant, Junior Consultant, Senior Consultant, Lead Consultant, and Finance Analyst vacancies have been announced through the NHSRC Recruitment 2026.

qualification.education_qualification: Post-graduation or higher qualification in public health, Community health, Preventive & Social Medicine, MBBS/BAMS/BHMS/BDS/Psychology with Post Graduate Degree in Public Health or Hospital/Health Administration, MBBS/BDS/AYUSH with Master's in Social Work/MD/PhD/MPH/MBA (Health Management)/Post Graduate Diploma in Hospital and Health Management/MBA in Rural Management, MBBS with Public Health post graduate qualifications (MD/MPH) or equivalent with 7+ years experience
qualification.details: Finance Analyst: MBA (Finance) or Post Graduate Diploma in Business Management/ Post Graduate Diploma in Business Analytics (Finance) from a recognized institution or a Master Degree in Commerce/ Economics from a recognized university. Consultant-Public Health Policy & Planning (NHM-I, MoH&FW): Post-graduation or higher qualification in public health, Community health, Preventive & Social Medicine. Junior Consultant, Knowledge Management Division (KMD): MBBS/BAMS/BHMS/BDS/Psychology with Post Graduate Degree (fulltime course) or higher qualification in Public Health, or Hospital or Health Administration. Junior Consultant-Public Health Policy & Planning (NHM-I, MoH&FW): Post-graduation or higher qualification in public health, Community health, Preventive & Social Medicine. Senior Consultant - Community Processes, NHSRC: MBBS/BDS/AYUSH with Master's in Social Work (specialisation in Medical Social work)/MD/PhD/MPH/MBA (Health Management/Health Administration)/Post Graduate Diploma in Hospital and Health Management/MBA in Rural Management (specialisation in health and WASH). Lead Consultant - Child Health (M&E and Documentation): MBBS with Public Health post graduate qualifications (MD/MPH) or Non MBBS with public health post graduate qualification (MD/MPH) with relevant degree BDS/BHMS/BAMS/Social Science/Social Work/Health Management.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 28-07-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of NHSRC to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: NHSRC Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the NHSRC Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. content_sections.heading: NHSRC Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Finance Analyst: MBA (Finance) or Post Graduate Diploma in Business Management/ Post Graduate Diploma in Business Analytics (Finance) from a recognized institution or a Master Degree in Commerce/ Economics from a recognized university. Consultant-Public Health Policy & Planning (NHM-I, MoH&FW): Post-graduation or higher qualification in public health, Community health, Preventive & Social Medicine. Junior Consultant, Knowledge Management Division (KMD): MBBS/BAMS/BHMS/BDS/Psychology with Post Graduate Degree (fulltime course) or higher qualification in Public Health, or Hospital or Health Administration. Junior Consultant-Public Health Policy & Planning (NHM-I, MoH&FW): Post-graduation or higher qualification in public health, Community health, Preventive & Social Medicine. Senior Consultant - Community Processes, NHSRC: MBBS/BDS/AYUSH with Master's in Social Work (specialisation in Medical Social work)/MD/PhD/MPH/MBA (Health Management/Health Administration)/Post Graduate Diploma in Hospital and Health Management/MBA in Rural Management (specialisation in health and WASH). Lead Consultant - Child Health (M&E and Documentation): MBBS with Public Health post graduate qualifications (MD/MPH) or Non MBBS with public health post graduate qualification (MD/MPH) with relevant degree BDS/BHMS/BAMS/Social Science/Social Work/Health Management.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online on or before 28-07-2026. To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section. Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of NHSRC to complete the application. Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details. Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format. Pay the application fee online, if applicable. Review all details carefully before submitting the form. Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for NHSRC Consultant posts?
faq.answer: The last date to apply online for the NHSRC Consultant posts is 28th July 2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What is the application fee for NHSRC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive, as per the provided information.

faq.question: Q3. What is the selection process for NHSRC Consultant positions?
faq.answer: The selection process for the NHSRC Consultant positions will primarily involve a Written Test, as indicated in the official notification details.

faq.question: Q4. Which organization is conducting this recruitment?
faq.answer: The recruitment is being conducted by the National Health Systems Resource Centre (NHSRC).

faq.question: Q5. What is the job type for these consultant positions?
faq.answer: The job type for these consultant positions is contractual.

faq.question: Q6. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The link to download the official notification PDF can be found in the "Important Links" section of this article.

faq.question: Q7. What are the minimum qualifications required for the Lead Consultant - Child Health post?
faq.answer: For the Lead Consultant - Child Health post, candidates need an MBBS with Public Health post graduate qualifications (MD/MPH) or Non-MBBS with public health post graduate qualification (MD/MPH) along with a relevant degree like BDS/BHMS/BAMS/Social Science/Social Work/Health Management.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary range for Junior Consultant posts?
faq.answer: The salary range for Junior Consultant posts is Rs. 40,000/- to Rs. 70,000/- per month.

faq.question: Q9. Can candidates apply offline for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, candidates must apply online for the NHSRC Consultant posts. Offline applications will not be accepted.
